name: ScorePlay
tagline: AI-powered media hub that automatically tags and distributes sports content for 200+ teams and leagues in real time.
why: FIBA-backed and trusted by 200+ sports organizations globally, ScorePlay solves the real-time content distribution problem that every major sports property faces. Their January 2025 Series A at $13M total validates the enterprise demand — and as media fragmentation accelerates, owning the content infrastructure layer is a strong moat.

name: Raidium
tagline: The 'GPT of radiology' — an AI-native foundation model that reads CT scans and MRIs in seconds, the way a radiologist would.
why: Founded by a radiologist and a data scientist from Owkin, Raidium built the world's first 3D large vision-language model for medical imaging. Their €16M seed co-led by Newfund and Kurma (Nov 2024) — with EIC backing — puts them at the frontier of applied clinical AI. Radiology is one of the highest-value, most AI-ready specialties in healthcare.

name: Lempire
tagline: A bootstrapped suite of B2B growth tools — lemlist, Taplio, Tweet Hunter — built to help businesses scale through outreach and content.
why: Guillaume Moubeche scaled Lempire to $45M ARR with a 90-person team and $10M EBITDA — zero primary VC. The portfolio model (multiple SaaS tools under one brand) is rare and defensible, and the build-in-public playbook has created an outsized distribution engine that most funded competitors can't replicate.

name: GrowSurf
tagline: Plug-and-play referral program software that turns your existing customers into your best acquisition channel.
why: Kevin Yun grew GrowSurf to $831K ARR with six employees and zero VC funding — profitable from day one in a market dominated by well-funded competitors. A textbook example of disciplined, product-led bootstrapping: the company publishes revenue publicly and compounds through word-of-mouth of the very product it sells.

name: Robeauté
tagline: A grain-of-rice-sized microrobot that navigates inside the brain, giving surgeons precision access to treat tumors and neurological conditions.
why: One of the most technically audacious European medtech bets in years. Backed by Plural, Cherry Ventures, and Kindred Ventures, with Brainlab (a leading surgical device maker) as strategic co-investor — dramatically de-risking the clinical and commercialization path. CEO Bertrand Duplat previously founded and sold Virtools to Dassault Systèmes.

name: Pro Padel League
tagline: The first professional padel league in North America, with 10 teams across the US, Canada, and Mexico.
why: Padel is the world's fastest-growing racket sport, with 30M+ players globally and explosive US momentum. PPL has first-mover advantage on the professional league structure, a Charlotte Hornets co-chairman-led Series A, and Gary Vaynerchuk and Left Lane Capital on cap table. The 2026 season opens in NYC this July.

name: Somnee
founder: Dr. Matthew Walker, Dr. Robert Knight, Tim Rosa
tagline: AI-powered sleep headband that reads your brainwaves and delivers personalized electrical stimulation to guide you into deep sleep faster.
why: Co-founded by the world's most cited sleep scientist — Dr. Matthew Walker, author of Why We Sleep — alongside UC Berkeley neuroscientists and ex-Fitbit exec Tim Rosa. NBA teams are already using it for athlete recovery. Khosla-led with Marc Benioff's TIME Ventures and the Adidas family fund. Rare convergence of scientific credibility and consumer distribution.

name: Mimo
tagline: All-in-one financial management platform for SMBs: get paid, pay suppliers, and see your real-time cash flow — all in one place.
why: Henrik Grim scaled Capchase across Europe as GM, then had Northzone (his former employer as an investor) lead Mimo's seed round — a signal of rare conviction. Mimo attacks the fragmented SMB payments stack with a simple payments-in / payments-out model that maps to how small businesses actually think about money.

name: Boardy
tagline: An AI that calls you on the phone, learns who you are, and makes warm introductions to the exact right people — automatically.
why: Boardy famously closed its own $8M seed round autonomously — the AI conducted investor calls without Andrew in the room. That's the ultimate product dogfood story. Creandum-led, with a genuinely novel phone-first UX for professional networking that no other product has attempted at scale.

name: Dust
tagline: AI agent platform that connects to your company's tools (Slack, Notion, GitHub) and deploys purpose-built assistants for every team function.
why: Stanislas Polu spent years on OpenAI's research team before co-founding Dust with former Alan Head of Product Gabriel Hubert. Sequoia backed them from seed through Series A — rare multi-round conviction from the world's top AI-focused VC. 1,000+ enterprise customers including Alan, Qonto, and Doctolib with €2M ARR growing triple digits.

name: Output Sports
founder: Dr. Martin O'Reilly, Dr. Darragh Whelan
tagline: A single wearable sensor that gives strength coaches and sports scientists lab-grade athlete performance data — anywhere, anytime.
why: Output Sports tech drove medal-winning performances at the 2024 Paris Olympics, including Ireland's first-ever swimming gold. Captured 30 million athlete measurements globally in 2024. A UCD spinout backed by Atlantic Bridge and Enterprise Ireland, now expanding into the US after proving elite adoption at the highest level of global sport.

name: Lago
tagline: Open-source usage-based billing infrastructure for SaaS — the developer-friendly alternative to Stripe Billing, built for AI-era pricing models.
why: Two early Qonto employees (Anh-Tho was employee #1) building the billing layer every usage-based SaaS eventually needs to own. YC S21, backed by FirstMark and SignalFire. As AI products shift to consumption pricing, Lago's open-source moat and ~$100M valuation at Series A suggests massive headroom ahead.

name: Cal.com
tagline: Open-source scheduling infrastructure that lets any company embed calendar booking natively — without sending users to a third-party tool.
why: Cal.com hit 50,000+ GitHub stars and became the go-to open-source Calendly alternative in under two years. Backed by 776 (Alexis Ohanian) and Obvious Ventures, with a community-driven GTM that dramatically lowers CAC. Peer Richelsen's previous company was acquired by WeWork's Flatiron School before he turned his attention to the scheduling problem.

name: Graza
tagline: Single-origin Spanish olive oil in a squeezy bottle — designed to be cooked with, not saved for a special occasion.
why: Hit $100K in revenue their first week, $19.7M by year two, $48M+ by year three — all on roughly $1M in seed capital. The genius was the product itself: a squeeze bottle that makes premium olive oil approachable and daily. Now in Whole Foods and Target. One of the most capital-efficient DTC brand-building stories in recent memory.

name: Fishwife
tagline: Beautifully tinned, sustainably sourced fish — turning a forgotten pantry staple into something you're proud to put on the table.
why: Almost single-handedly revived the $2.6B US tinned fish category through obsessive branding and sustainability storytelling. Bootstrapped and angel-backed, now in Whole Foods nationally. Becca Millstein made Inc.'s Female Founders 250 list and appeared on Shark Tank. A masterclass in repositioning a commodity category through brand.

name: Vacation Inc.
founder: Marty Bell, Lach Hall, Dakota Green
tagline: Award-winning sunscreen that smells like a vacation — on a mission to make SPF the most desirable product in your beach bag.
why: Built an $80M retail sales brand on pure creative marketing and nostalgia — no complex tech, just the right product, aesthetic, and brand story. $6M Series A led by Silas Capital, VMG Partners later came in at a $210M valuation. Turned a commoditized SPF category into a lifestyle object. Proof that taste and brand conviction can build a defensible consumer business.

name: Polar
tagline: Merchant-of-record payment infrastructure for AI-native developers — handle billing, taxes, and subscriptions globally from day one.
why: Founded by ex-Shopify Director of Product (and Tictail founder, acquired by Shopify in 2018), Polar's $10M seed was led by Accel with angels including Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke, Vercel's Guillermo Rauch, and Supabase's Paul Copplestone. 16,000 developers on the platform with average sales growing 120%+ month-on-month. Perfectly timed for the wave of solo AI developers who need Stripe-grade infrastructure without the complexity.

name: Resend
tagline: The modern email API for developers — beautiful transactional emails that actually land in the inbox, not spam.
why: 200,000+ developers signed up in 18 months, powering millions of emails per day for brands like Warner Brothers and Decathlon. $18M Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz (Dec 2024) validates the thesis that developer email infrastructure is broken and ripe for a clean-sheet rebuild. YC W23 pedigree and obsessive developer experience design make Resend the Stripe of email.

name: Cavela
tagline: The world's first AI product sourcing agent — automating supplier matching, negotiation, and order delivery for consumer brands.
why: Anthony Sardain grew up across Southeast Asian trade hubs in a family with three generations in materials trade, then built an AI agent that reduces 1,000 sourcing touch points to 10. Seed co-led by XYZ Venture Capital and Susa Ventures (Nov 2025). 200,000+ vetted suppliers across 40+ countries, cutting production costs by an average of 35%. With tariff uncertainty reshaping global supply chains, Cavela's timing is surgical.

name: Liveblocks
tagline: Real-time collaboration infrastructure that lets any product team add Figma-like multiplayer features — cursors, comments, notifications — in hours, not months.
why: Boldstart-led with Seedcamp and angels from Figma, GitHub, Vercel, and Segment backing the bet. Liveblocks is the picks-and-shovels play for the collaboration economy — every SaaS product eventually needs multiplayer, and building it from scratch is brutally hard. Two French founders quietly building the infrastructure layer that makes every app feel alive.

name: Peripheral Labs
tagline: AI-powered neural rendering that turns standard broadcast cameras into photorealistic 3D volumetric sports replays — let fans fly into the action from any angle.
why: Self-driving car computer vision engineers from University of Toronto who figured out how to do what Intel TrueView does with 100+ cameras using just 32 — a 3x hardware reduction that makes the technology economically viable for any league, not just the NFL. Khosla-led seed (Dec 2025). The intersection of autonomous vehicle AI and sports media is a category-creating bet.

name: Ghia
tagline: A beautifully designed non-alcoholic aperitif that gives you the ritual of a cocktail without the hangover — the Aesop of the sober-curious movement.
why: Ex-Glossier Head of Retail Melanie Masarin raised her first $900K from Emily Weiss and her network in a single week, then scaled Ghia to $7M+ revenue and a $50M valuation. Appeared on Shark Tank Season 14. Retail sales up 132% in 2024. Ghia rides the massive sober-curious wave ($1.3T non-alcoholic beverage market) with brand design and taste that make not drinking feel aspirational, not restrictive.

name: MUD\WTR
tagline: Mushroom-based coffee alternative with cacao, chai, and adaptogens — designed to give you energy and focus without the jitters or crash.
why: Shane Heath put $25K of his home down-payment money into building MUD\WTR, raised just $1M in seed from friends and family, then scaled to $60M+ projected revenue by year three and 130,000+ monthly subscribers. No Series A, no growth equity, no dilution — just a single product that tapped into the anti-caffeine-dependency zeitgeist. One of the most capital-efficient consumer brand stories of the decade.

name: Sycai Medical
founder: Sara Toledano, Javier García, Júlia Rodríguez-Comas
tagline: AI-powered medical device that detects early-stage cancer and pre-malignant lesions in the abdomen from standard imaging — no invasive test required.
why: Top-funded European Medical Diagnostics seed in 2025, led by LUMO Labs and Ship2B Ventures, plus a €5M Horizon Europe grant. CE-marked under MDR for pancreatic cancer detection (summer 2024) with a successful first clinical trial in 2023. Pancreatic cancer has a 12% five-year survival rate largely because it's caught too late — Sycai's non-invasive AI screening could fundamentally change early detection economics.

name: TORTUS
tagline: AI medical scribe that listens to doctor-patient conversations and writes clinical notes, lab requests, and referral letters — so clinicians can focus on care, not paperwork.
why: Founded by a practising cardiologist and a machine learning engineer, TORTUS became the first ambient AI company to achieve NHS DTAC compliance and deploy in an NHS hospital — a regulatory moat that US competitors can't easily replicate. Khosla-led $4.2M seed (Feb 2024) with Entrepreneur First backing. In a market where Abridge raised $300M at scale, TORTUS is the early-stage UK bet on the same thesis with NHS distribution built in.

name: Dazzle
tagline: Next-generation AI personal assistant that closes the gap between what people want to do and what they can do with technology — making AI simpler, smarter, and more approachable.
why: When a former Google VP and Yahoo CEO puts her own capital into a seed round at a $35M valuation and attracts Forerunner's Kirsten Green (who discovered Warby Parker, Chime, and Dollar Shave Club) and Kleiner Perkins — it's a signal worth paying attention to. Mayer's deep product instincts and Rolodex give Dazzle distribution advantages most seed-stage consumer AI startups can only dream of.
